title: Cheilanthes microphylla
taxonomy: Cheilanthes microphylla is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae, commonly known as the wood fern family. It belongs to the genus Cheilanthes, which includes various species of rock ferns. The genus Cheilanthes is further classified within the family Pteridaceae, and more broadly, it is a member of the class Polypodiopsida, which includes all ferns and their allies. This classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships. As a member of the Pteridophyta division, Cheilanthes microphylla exhibits characteristics typical of vascular plants that reproduce via spores.
uses: Cheilanthes microphylla, also known as the microphyllous lip fern, is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever and respiratory issues. The leaves of the plant are often harvested and used in teas or infusions to promote well-being. Cheilanthes microphylla is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and swelling. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of this plant species.
